title: Composio Documentation
summary: Meta-tool architecture avoids loading 1000+ definitions into context 5-server MCP setup consumes ~55K tokens before conversation starts
creator_view: Meta-tool architecture avoids loading 1000+ definitions into context 5-server MCP setup consumes ~55K tokens before conversation starts

candidates.title: Meta-tool pattern for dynamic tool discovery
candidates.description: Instead of loading all tool definitions, provide a small set of meta tools (search, get-schema, execute). Agent discovers at runtime. Solves context window bloat. Applicable to JASON-OS: if the OS has 72+ skills, a meta-skill for discovery would be more efficient than loading all skill definitions.
candidates.extraction_action: Evaluate: could JASON-OS use a meta-skill pattern? Currently all skills load via SKILL.md reading. At 100+ skills, a search-first approach avoids context bloat.

candidates.title: Native vs MCP tradeoff framework with token cost data
candidates.description: Native: context control + interception. MCP: simplicity + client-agnostic. 55K token overhead for 5 MCP servers. Decision framework for when to use which.
candidates.extraction_action: Measure current MCP token overhead (3 servers). Add to AGENT_ORCHESTRATION.md or MCP reference doc as decision criteria.

candidates.title: llms.txt standard for AI-readable documentation
candidates.description: Standardized llms.txt and llms-full.txt at site root for LLM consumption. If JASON-OS publishes documentation, this format makes it AI-discoverable.
candidates.extraction_action: Park for JASON-OS documentation strategy. Evaluate llms.txt format for skill/agent docs.

candidates.title: Event trigger dual model (webhook + polling) for agent reactivity
candidates.description: Webhook for real-time capable services, polling for those without. User-scoped, account-bound. Pattern for making agents event-driven rather than invocation-driven.
candidates.extraction_action: Park for later. Relevant if SoNash adds event-driven features (meeting reminders, journal prompts) or JASON-OS needs external event reactions.

site.title: Composio Documentation
site.description: Platform powering 1000+ toolkits for AI agent tool integration
site.author: Composio

absence_patterns: No Claude Code skill integration documented despite skills.sh presence
No pricing or rate limit information
No comparison to building custom MCP servers
key_claims: Meta-tool architecture avoids loading 1000+ definitions into context
5-server MCP setup consumes ~55K tokens before conversation starts
Native tools give precise context control; MCP gives simplicity
Session-scoped state preserves context across meta-tool calls
Remote workbench provides persistent sandboxed execution for agents
